def: s.m. Refuge, shelter, asylum; protection, patronage, favour; resting-place, dwelling-place; alliance.
s.f. Course, current, flow, stream; revolution; course of mundane existence, transmigration; the world.

s.m. A collection, aggregation, heap, quantity, crowd; the aggregation of primitive atoms, primary formation; conformation, configuration; form, figure, shape, fabrication, construction; common place of abode, vicinity, neighbourhood; a place where four roads meet; a place, station; position, situation; spot, mark, sign; standing still, stopping; dying, death.
s.f. (local) The season of spring; name of a month.

ety: Prk. संतो; S. सन्तस्, base सत्, part. of अस् 'to be'
def: adj. & s.m. Being, existing; essential; good, virtuous, pious; right, proper; excellent; steady, respectable, venerable; — a pious or venerable man, a saint; a good or simple man
more: n. m. S. सत. 1. A devotee; saint. 1. Paisā ṭakā jo pās thā, sab sādh santoṅ ko dīyā, Sab kuchh ṭajā Har dhyān meṅ, aur nām Har kā le līyā . Nazir. His pence to monks and perfect holy men he gives up all, Abandoned all, on Har intent, on Har's name he doth call. 2. Jab āyā dehī kā ant jaisā gadhā vaisā sant . Prov. When perishes this earthy frame Or ass or saint it's all the same. 2. A simple good man.

adj. Devour; pious (Sant. jan ). Cham-drishtī sansār hai, ātam-drishṭī sant, Bramh-drishṭi birle bhaë jinkā ād na ant . The world the flesh beholds, the seer the soul can see, Few Brahm have seen who ever was and e'er shall be.
1. s. m. A kind of devotee, a saint. 2. adj. Pious, virtuous.
subs: s.f. The state or condition of a saint, saintliness; venerableness
s.f., and sant-samāj, s.m. The assembly of the good (the term is applied chiefly to the Hindū ascetics regarded as to their corporate capacity)
s.m. The intercourse or union of the pious.

def: s.f. = شانتي śānti, q.v.
v. a. 1. To gum; paste. 2. To join (threads); to splice; tack. 3. To twist. Rassī sāṅṭnā . 4. To weave. 5. ( sāṅṭ lenā ) To bring over to one's side; to buy off.

part. (f. -ā), Appeased, allayed, calmed, alleviated, pacified, hushed, calm, tranquil, free from passions, contented, undisturbed; meek, humbled; put a stop to, put an end to, destroyed, killed; deceased, dead; ceased; — s.m. One whose passions are subdued, an ascetic, one absorbed in meditation on the deity
more: adj. Appeased ( ṭhanḍā 6, 7). shānt k. , v. a. To appease; calm; assuage; pacify.
subs: (˚ta+āt˚), adj. & s.m.f. Of a resigned or composed spirit, calm-minded, composed; — a person of a composed or resigned disposition
s.m. One whose fever is alleviated
s.m. (f. -ā), A person of subdued passions, or of tranquil disposition
v.t. To appease, allay, calm, assuage, &c. (see śānt).

def: part. corr. of شانت śānt, q.v.
more: n. f. 1. ( sanṭī ) A flail. 2. A knot. Is kapṛe meṅ sāṅṭ paṛī huī hai . 3. ( sāṅṭ bāṅṭ, sānṭ gāṅṭ ) Collusion ( sāzish ). Inkī sāṅṭ se chorī huī .
adj. Calm, tranquil, pacified, appeased. سانت کرنا sānt karnā, To appease, pacify. سانت هونا sānt honā, To be appeased or pacified.

def: s.f. Joining, sticking; contact, union, junction, cohesion; attachment, liaison; confederacy, collusion; — a knot; — a flail
more: s. f. 1. Joining, sticking. 2. Confederacy, collusion. 3. A stick for threshing corn with, a flail.
subs: (or sāṭ-bāṭ), and sāṅṭ-gāṅṭ, s.f. Combining, leaguing, &c.; combination, league, confederacy, collusion, intrigue.

ety: sāṅṭ, q.v.+अओ = S. अ+कः; cf. soṅṭā
def: s.m. A stick or whip (for driving oxen, or for urging an elephant to fight); a goad; a spur; a sugar-cane.
more: s. m. 1. A stick, or whip, with which elephants are beaten at the time of fight. 2. A spur.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Wicked, depraved; miserly.
more: n. m. A piece of wood in geṛī , q. v.
adj. 1. Depraved. 2. Miserly. See سنٿهہ sanṭh.

ety: prob. fr. S. अस्यत्, part. of अस् 'to throw'
def: s.m. The piece of wood which is thrown in the game called geṛī, q.v.
n. m. 1. A stick with which oxen are driven. 2. A goad with which the driver urges his elephant to fight.
s.m. 'The giver of happiness or joy to the virtuous,' an epithet of Vishṇu.

s.m. & f. = H سنتانا सन्ताना santānā, s.m. Extension, expansion, spreading; lineage, race, descent, family; offspring, progeny, children; a son or daughter; — one of the five trees of Indra's paradise.
more: s. (dakh.) Circumcision: see سنت sunnat, sign. 1.

def: s.f. The act of appeasing or reconciling, conciliation, reconcilement; palliation; mildness
more: s. f. Palliation, consolation, act of appeasing. سنتناکرنا santanā karnā, To appease, to still, to quiet, to lull, to comfort.
subs: (-kī), To appease, to conciliate, to still, quiet, lull, comfort.

def: s.m. = S سنتان सन्तान san-tān, s.m. & f. Extension, expansion, spreading; lineage, race, descent, family; offspring, progeny, children; a son or daughter; — one of the five trees of Indra's paradise.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Of or relating to offspring; having offspring; — one who has offspring, a parent.
s.f. Froth, foam; cream; coagulated milk; custard; a cobweb; the blade of a knife or sword.
Shantanu, a mythological king in the mahābharata; a male given name, Shantanu, from Sanskrit
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit शंतनु (śáṃtanu). Compare Persian شانتانو (šântânu).

def: alternative spelling of شَنْتَانُو (śantanu)
adj. Possessing a body, embodied.
s.m. Great heat, burning heat; inflammation; fire; affliction, pain, anguish, distress, sorrow, misery; passion; resentment; remorse, penitence, repentance; penance
more: n. m. 1. Heat; burning heat. 2. Affliction; distress ( pīṛā , 2.). santāp k. yā denā . To afflict ( dukh denā ).
s. m. Sorrow, pain, affliction, distress, misery.
subs: and santāp karnā (-kā), To inflict pain or misery (on), to afflict, &c.

def: adj. Made hot, heated, inflamed; consumed; tormented, pained, afflicted, distressed; sad, sorrowful; impassioned; penitent, suffering remorse; — santāpī, s.m. An afflicted person, &c.
adj. Made hot, heated, inflamed; consumed; tormented, pained, afflicted, distressed; sad, sorrowful; impassioned; penitent, suffering remorse; — santāpī, s.m. An afflicted person, &c.
part. Greatly heated or inflamed, red-hot, glowing; burnt, burnt up; vexed, inflamed with passion; suffering great pain or anguish, tormented, distressed, afflicted, pained, wretched.
s.m. corr. of sangtarā, q.v. s.m. A kind of orange; (met.) breast of a woman.
wety: Borrowed from Portuguese Sintra (“a town in Lisboa, Portugal”). Compare semantically Italian portogallo (“sweet orange”, literally “Portugal”) and Gujarati સંતરું (santrũ).

def: s.m. Crossing over, passing over; being saved.

s.f. Extent, expanse, width; continuity; a continuous line, row, range; uninterrupted succession, descent; lineage, race, offspring, posterity, progeny; a son, a daughter; a number of people, multitude.
part. Continued, continuous, uninterrupted, continual, lasting, perpetual, eternal.
s.f. = S شانتتو शान्तत्व śāntatva, s.m. Quietness, calmness, quietism; meekness.

s.f. Extent, expanse, width; continuity; a continuous line, row, range; uninterrupted succession, descent; lineage, race, offspring, posterity, progeny; a son, a daughter; a number of people, multitude.
s.m. = S شانتتا शान्तता śāntatā, s.f. Quietness, calmness, quietism; meekness.
adv. In the being; in the presence (of); during, whilst (syn. hote, or hote-huʼe, or huʼe: the word, in this sense, would appear to be peculiar to the Benares dialect as used by panḍits)
subs: ( = santī, q.v.), In the place (of, -ke) instead (of), in lieu (of), in substitution (for).

def: = H سانتي सान्ती sāntī, See santī, and sante.
s.f. = sāṅṭ, q.v.
adj. Wicked, depraved; roguish, crafty; miserly.
s.f. Reading, instruction, a lesson.
s.f. Thigh (syn. rān).
Santali (language)
s.m. = sāṭhī, q.v. s.m. Companion, comrade, associate, attendant, follower; ally, accomplice, supporter; — adv. More
subs: v.t. To make a companion of.

def: v.t. = sāṅṭnā, q.v. v.t. To stick, cause to adhere or cohere, to gum, paste; to join, unite; to combine; to join threads, to splice; to twist (rope, &c.); to weave; to attach (a person) to oneself, win (one) over or away (from)

v.n. = saṭnā, q.v. v.t. = sāṅṭnā, and saṭānā, qq.v. v.t. To stick, cause to adhere or cohere, to gum, paste; to join, unite; to combine; to join threads, to splice; to twist (rope, &c.); to weave; to attach (a person) to oneself, win (one) over or away (from)
s.f. = sāthrī, q.v. s.f. A small carpet or mat on which a Hindū sits to worship.
s.f. = شانتي śānti, q.v.
s.f. A flail; a small stick or goad, &c. (see sāṅṭā).
s.f. A succedaneum; a substitute; — postpn. Instead (of, -kī), &c. ( = sante, q.v.).
more: n. f. A twig. sanṭī mārnā , v. a. To strike with a twig or lash; to whip. Mīyāṅ jī ne sanṭī mārī .

def: s.f. Quiet, quietness, tranquillity, calmness, peace, ease, comfort, rest, repose, remission; absence of passion, indifference to objects of pleasure or pain, stoicism; pacification, alleviation; consolation, &c.; cessation; preservation; pardon
more: n. f. 1. Quietude; repose ( āsūdgī , 1). 2. Pardon ( chhimā , 1).
subs: s.m. 'Tranquillizing section,' name of the twelfth book of the Mahā-bhārata

adj. & s.m. (f. -trī, and śānti-dāyak (f. -ikā) = śānti-dāʼī, adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Giving tranquillity, tranquillizing, imparting rest or repose, soothing, peace-giving; — a tranquillizer, comforter, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit शान्ति (śānti). Equivalent of شانت (śānt, “quiet”) + ـی (-ī). Doublet of چین (cain).

def: = H سانتے सान्ते sānṭe, See santī, and sante.
s.f. A switch, a cane, a thin stick.
part. adj. Consoled, comforted, &c. (see śānt).

v.t. To stick, cause to adhere or cohere, to gum, paste; to join, unite; to combine; to join threads, to splice; to twist (rope, &c.); to weave; to attach (a person) to oneself, win (one) over or away (from)
more: (caus. of سٿنا saṭnā ) v. a. To join thread, to join, splice, to stick together.

def: s.m. = santosh, q.v. s.m. Contentedness, content, satisfaction, gratification, pleasure, joy, delight, happiness; satiety; patience; — the thumb and forefinger
more: (or santosh̤ ) s. m. Content, patience, satiety, satisfaction, pleasure, gratification. سنتوکهہ کرنا santokh̤ karnā, To be patient, to be content.
subs: (or santokh) karnā, To exercise contentedness, to be content; to practice patience, to be patient; to take comfort.

def: n. m. S. सन्तोष Satisfaction; content ( dhīraj , 2). 1. Gaū-dhan, gaj-dhan, kanak-dhan, ratan-khān, bahu khān, Jab āyā santokh-dhan, sab dhan dhūl samān . Dohā. Be't in cattle, elephants, and gold, and mines, and gems, The breast which once contentment fills all other wealth contemns. 2. Māriye āsā sāṅpnī jin daṅseu sansār! [Bhoj. song. Tākī aukhad tosh hai, bahu gur mantr bichār . Perish the snake desire whose fangs the world hath stung, Its cure contentment is all sages' charms among. santokh k. , v. a. To be patient or content.

adj. = santoshī, or santushṭ, q.v. adj. = santushṭ, q.v. part. adj. Satisfied (with, par), content, well-pleased, delighted, gratified; consoled, comforted, at rest; patient, resigned; at ease, comfortable.

adj. = santoshī, or santushṭ, q.v. adj. = santushṭ, q.v. part. adj. Satisfied (with, par), content, well-pleased, delighted, gratified; consoled, comforted, at rest; patient, resigned; at ease, comfortable.
more: adj. 1. Patient; contented. Tīn pāo kī tīn pakāīṅ, savā-ser kī ek, Jeṭh niputtā tīnoṅ khā gayā, maiṅ santokhan ek! Wom. Three cakes I baked one quarter each, and one a quarter o'er a seer, The elder brother, childless be! ate three, me one contented sheer. 2. Comfortable; well-off.

def: s.m. Contentedness, content, satisfaction, gratification, pleasure, joy, delight, happiness; satiety; patience; — the thumb and forefinger
subs: (or santokh) karnā, To exercise contentedness, to be content; to practice patience, to be patient; to take comfort.

def: adj. = santushṭ, q.v. part. adj. Satisfied (with, par), content, well-pleased, delighted, gratified; consoled, comforted, at rest; patient, resigned; at ease, comfortable.
n. m. Cor. of sentinel.
part. adj. Satisfied (with, par), content, well-pleased, delighted, gratified; consoled, comforted, at rest; patient, resigned; at ease, comfortable.
s.f. Complete satisfaction, contentment, gratification ( = santosh).
s.f. Complete satisfaction, contentment, gratification ( = santosh).
s.m. Conciliation, reconcilement; mildness; appeasing; speaking kindly and in a conciliatory manner; friendly salutation and inquiry.
s.m. Level ground on the top or edge of a mountain, table-land; summit, ridge; top, end, point; precipice; a shoot, sprout; a forest, wood; a road; a gale of wind; a sage.
adv. Along with (one's) younger brother.
adj. Attended by favourable circumstances.
s.m. Tooth-powder.

adj. (in Gram.) Pronounced with a nasal sound, nasal (the term is applied to any letter that has anuswār or ćandra-vindu over it; also to the fifth letter of each of the first five classes of consonants in the Nāgarī).
s.m. = sān, 'whetstone'; — s.f. corr. of śānti; qq.v.
s.m. = sāmāk, q.v. (cf. sāmā). adj. & s.m. Belonging to the Sāma-veda; — one who chants the Sāma-veda.
more: s. m. The name of a very small grain. (Panicum frumentaceum: Roxb. ).

def: s.m. Conversation, discourse; information, intelligence, news, report; history, story; communication (of news, &c.); assent, agreement, conformity, correspondence, sameness.
s.m. = sāmak and sāṅwā, qq.v. adj. & s.m. Belonging to the Sāma-veda; — one who chants the Sāma-veda.
adj. = next, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀲𑀸𑀫𑀮 (sāmala), from Sanskrit श्यामल (śyāmalá).

def: s.m. An epithet of Krishṇ ( = sāṅʼolī-ṣūrat).
s.m. = sāwan, q.v. s.m. The fourth Hindū month, July-August (beginning when the sun enters Cancer: its full moon is near Śravaṇā or α Aquilæ)
subs: s.m. Sunshine and rain; — a kind of lattice-work; — a kind of firework
s.f. The constant showers of Sāwan
'Flourishing in summer and not fading in autumn'; continuing in the same state or condition.

def: s.m. = sāmāk, q.v. (cf. sāmā). adj. & s.m. Belonging to the Sāma-veda; — one who chants the Sāma-veda.
more: s. m. The name of a very small grain. (Panicum frumentaceum: Roxb. ).

def: s.f. = sāwanī, q.v.

ety: saṅwār˚ = Prk. संवारे(इ) = S. संवारय (ति), caus. of rt. सम्+वृ
def: v.t. To prepare, to construct; to dress, decorate, adorn, deck; to arrange, methodize, regulate, rectify, correct, mend, improve.
adj. & s.m. = sāwant, q.v. adj. & s.m. Brave, warlike, heroic; — a leader, general; warrior, hero, champion.
s.f. Preparation, arrangement, regulation, rectification, correction, amendment, &c.
more: n. f. 1. Amendment ( durustī , 4—6 and islāh ). Mār pīchhe saṅvār hī hai? First mar, then make! (First slay, then raise to life?). 2. Wom. Curse; malediction. Allāh-māre, tujh ko Khudā kī saṅvār ho! Mah. Wom. O heaven-accursed! God mend you (destroy you).

def: s.m. Restraint, self-control, forbearance, continence.

ety: saṅwār˚ = Prk. संवारे(इ) = S. संवारय (ति), caus. of rt. सम्+वृ
def: v.t. To prepare, to construct; to dress, decorate, adorn, deck; to arrange, methodize, regulate, rectify, correct, mend, improve.
more: v. a. 1. See ārāstah k . and ṭhīk k. , (2—4). 1. Koī sakhī laṅgaṭe, koī lugava saṅvarate, Ahe jāit gelan āpan āpan gharāvā re kī . Mag. Shiv's marriage. Some women naked were, and some their sheets about them drew, In sooth they one and all to their own houses quick withdrew. ☞ While the women were abusing the loathsome form in which the god bridegroom appeared, he caused serpents to appear under their clothes which they hastily threw off and ran naked to their homes. 2. Merī bigrī ko saṅvāro , Rām! Relieve my misery, O Ram! 2. To correct; amend; improve. Bolo, pyāre, Sītā Rām! Rām saṅvāre sab-kā kām! Mendicants. Call, friend, on Sītā Ram! who all things does for you, his name proclaim!
(caus. of سنورنا) v. a. To prepare, dress, deck, decorate, adjust, adorn, arrange, accomplish.

def: v.n. To be prepared; to be dressed, or decorated, or decked; to be arranged, &c. (see saṅ wārnā); to suit; to prosper; to improve; to become steady; to recover, to come right again.
more: v. n. S. संवैरण See saṅvārnā .
v. n. To be dressed, prepared, or arranged, to be decorated, to suit, prosper.

def: s.m. A year; an era; the era of Vikramāditya (reckoning from about 56¾ B.C.)
subs: (or sambat) bāṅdhnā, To establish an era; to mark or fix an epoch; to distinguish oneself by illustrious actions

def: adj. (dakh.) Thoughtless: see بي فکر be-fikr.
s.f. A covered cart ( = sāṅgī, q.v.). s.m. Mimic, actor, player.
more: n. f. A covered cart for passengers.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Of a dark or sallow complexion; dark, swarthy; sallow, brown, nut-brown; of handsome countenance; an epithet of Krishṇ
more: adj. S. श्यामल, Nut-brown. 1. Torī sāṅvlī sūrat, mohnī mūrat jīyā meṅ more bas gaī re . Song. Thumrī . Your brown complexion and enchanting shape have in my heart a dwelling made. 2. Sāṅvrī dekh-ke sūrat kisī matvālī kī, Go musalmān hūṅ, magar bolūṅgā 'jai Kālī kī! ' Whene 'er a nut-brown maid with eyes intoxicate I chance to see, Although a Moslem I'm constrained to cry 'to Kālī glory be!' sāṅvlā, yā sāṅvrā salonā, sāṅvlī salonī , adj. 1. Brown and saltish. 2. Rich brown. Sāṅvle salone haiṅ sharbat ko! (Fālsā grewia asiatica , Wat.) Delhi street cry. sāṅvrā, sāṅvalyā, sāṅolā , n. m. An epithet of Krishna. Sāṅore ne moko gārī ḍi, e daī, mai ne kachhu nā kahī . Brij. Wom. song.
adj. Of a dark or sallow (complexion).
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Brown and salty; rich brown; — piquant, pleasing
adj. & s.m. Having a sallow or a handsome countenance; — one who has a sallow countenance, &c., an epithet of Krishṇ.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀸𑀫𑀮 (sāmala), from Sanskrit श्यामल (śyāmalá).

adj. & s.m. Having a sallow or a handsome countenance; — one who has a sallow countenance, &c., an epithet of Krishṇ.

n. m. S. श्राद्ध The funeral rites of Hindus performed on certain days in honor of one's ancestors; obsequies of the dead. Āj kyā bāp kā sarādh kīyā thā? (Said of a feast). ekādash sarāddh . Presentation of offerings to the deceased on the 11th day after his decease, when Brahmans are fed, and the period of uncleanness for a Brahman terminates. pret sarāddh, dash pinḍ sarāddh , n. m. An offering of a pind or ball of rice to a person deceased by the next of kin, increasing the number daily for ten successive days until they amount to ten. sapinḍī sarāddh , n. m. barsī , n. f. The presentation of the pinḍ to the deceased and his progenitors in both lines on the first anniversary after one's death. māsik sarāddh , n. m. hāṅḍī , n. f. The monthly srādh performed for one year.

def: v.t. To praise, magnify, commend, applaud, celebrate, eulogize; to approve, appreciate, admire; — s.m. = sarāhan.
more: v. a. S. श्लाघा Pr. salāhā from शलघ to praise. 1. To praise; commend; applaud. Kadhī sarāhā man ne nāhīṅ, āchhā bhī khā-kar tan ne; Dukh is ghar māṅ sadā batāyā, sukhṛā bhī pā-kar tan ne! W. Rus. Wom. song. You never gave me any praise when you have had good fare, Of ills complaining e'er though you have had of joy your share. 2. To appreciate; admire. Kānī ko kaun sarāhve? Kānī kā bāvā . Prov. Whoe'er the one-eyed girl doth praise? Her father. 3. To flatter; butter; beslaver. Phūhaṛ kā mā/?/ sarāh sarāh khāīye . Prov. Flatter the fool and you may have her money.

ety: sarak˚ = Prk. सरक्क(इ), or सरक्के(इ), fr. S. सर+कृ; — cf. saṛaknā
def: v.n. To be moved, to be displaced; to move, shift, slip, get out of place; to stir, budge, get out of the way, remove; to flinch, draw back, recede, retire, give way, give place
more: v. n. 1. See sarkānā , (1, 2). 1. Nek eṅ saraki āi, yār! mohī kā jāṛo lagat hai! E. Wom. Ṭhumrī . A little nearer this side, love! I am so cold. 2. Andar chilvan, bāhar chilvan, bīch kalejā dhaṛke, Amīr Khusro yoṅ kaheṅ, woh do do uṅgal sarke . Riddle. Ans. Qaiṅchī . Scissors. Inside and out a curtain see, betwixt a fluttering heart, Its steps are jerking starts just fingers two apart. 2. To move off; turn aside; give way. Sarak, balamvā! morī biṅdyā hirānī, Sagri sejaṛyāṅ ḍhūṅḍat hūṅ, milat nāhīṅ . Song. Ṭhumrī . Just move a little husband, pray, my spangle's dropped somewhere, I've looked all o'er the bed, I cannot find it anywhere. 3. To draw back; recede; retire. 4. To slide; glide. Khule-bandoṅ huī āmad sahar kī, Uṭhā dāman ko shab āge se sarkī . Fidvī. The dawn unveiled appeared to view, Her skirt tucked in the night withdrew.
v. a. 1. To sniffle. 2. To gulp down. 3. To draw a sword from the scabbard.
v. n. To be moved, to remove, move, stir, to get out of the way, budge.
subs: v.n. (intens.), To move away, to get out of place, to slip, &c. ( = saraknā).

def: s.f. Name of the bitch of Indra; — name of the daughter of Daksh; and of the wife of Rāvan's brother.

see s.v. sar, 'head.' s.m. Essence, substance, best or choicest part (of a thing), quintessence; heart; marrow; cream; pith; gist; sap (of plants or trees); strength, vigour; valour, heroism, prowess; value, worth, excellence; wealth, goods, riches; curds; nectar; fresh butter; — manure; pus, matter; sickness, disease; dysentery; air, wind; iron; water; — a piece (at ćausar, or chess, &c.); a chess-board; — propriety, fitness; climax; — adj. Essential, substantial; vigorous, strong, hard excellent; good, sound; best; real, true, genuine; thoroughly proved, irrefragable
more: (from سر Head, and آمدن To come; q. d. arrived at the summit) adj. Perfect, complete, accomplished. 2. Master of any art, &c. 3. Chief.
subs: (˚ra+aṅśa), s.m. The best part (of a thing), substance, essence; epitome, summary; table of contents
adj. Consisting of the essence or best part (of a thing)
(-kā), To know the worth (of), to appreciate, to value, prize
adj. Extracting the essence (of a thing)
s.m. The act of extracting the essence or best part (of a thing)
s.m. Cow-shed, a kind of hut (in the jungle) used by cowherds
s.m. lit. 'Lord of essences,' the Almighty
adj. (f. -vatī), Having substance, substantial, strong; having pith or sap, pithy; fertile
adj. (f, -ā), Devoid of substance, unsubstantial; destitute of pith or sap, pithless, sapless.

def: s.m. (f. -ī), A dog
subs: (sār˚+ad˚), adj. & s.m. Dog-eating; — name of one of the hells of Hindū mythology.
